title: Why TanStack Wins
brief:
# Brief 6: Headless / Why TanStack is Hot

A flat-tech infographic visual concept on the TanStack "headless + composable" design philosophy. English labels only.

Central composition: a single bold heading-style element "Why TanStack Wins" not as text but as a strong visual focal point — a gear or puzzle of four layered rings.

Around the center, four cardinal points each holding a principle module:

Top: "Framework-Agnostic Core" - a small icon showing three stacked blocks (React, Vue, Solid) hovering over a single base. Tagline icon: a small "shared base" symbol.

Right: "Type-Safe by Design" - an icon of typed brackets <T> with a green check mark. Sub-tag: "refactor fearless".

Bottom: "Production-Grade Defaults" - icon of a shield with a check mark. Sub-tag: "stale-while-revalidate / retry / dedup".

Left: "No Vendor Lock-in" - an open padlock icon. Sub-tag: "OSS, independent".

A subtle dashed line connects all four principles back to the center, forming a tight ring.

In a corner (lower right), a small overlay: a horizontal bar chart-like visual showing "11.3B+ NPM Downloads", "391M+ Weekly", "124K+ GitHub Stars" as three small stat modules.

Color: tanstack mint #32E6E2, soft slate gray, off-white background, very subtle drop shadows.

Style: flat-tech-infographic, clean, document-friendly, generous whitespace.
prompt:
Create a polished article illustration titled 'Why TanStack Wins'. Diagram type: knowledge card. Use a flat technical infographic illustration, clean grouped modules, clear arrows, soft professional palette, concise bilingual labels, document-friendly composition. Make it suitable for insertion into engineering notes or a design document. Prefer a clear information hierarchy with section titles, concise Chinese/English labels, short notes, arrows, grouped modules, and strong readability. Avoid dense paragraph blocks. Language mode: en. Target aspect ratio preset: doc-hd. Mode: text-only. Content brief:
